title: Named variant enemies — fixed-affix elite identities
problem: Elite and rare monsters are generated with random affixes from a pool, which means every 'zombie' elite is statistically anonymous — the same creature class but with randomized modifiers that players can't anticipate. After enough runs, all elites blur together. Named variants provide memorable, fixed-affix identities that experienced players can recognise and prepare for.

lineage.spawnReason: gameplay depth: reuses existing affix infrastructure to create named enemy identities — no new systems, pure data + spawn-logic change

analysis: Named variants are pre-defined elite monster configurations: a baseId (existing monster def), a human-readable name, a fixed set of 2 affixes (forcedAffixes), a minimum floor before they can appear, and an HP bonus multiplier. On spawn, elite+ tier monsters have a 20% chance to pick from the eligible variants for the current floor rather than generating a random affix combo. The variant uses the base monster's stats + hpBonus scaling + the fixed affixes. All existing affix mechanics (resistance, hit modifiers, etc.) apply unchanged — the variant layer is purely a selection and naming mechanism on top of the existing system. Eight variants are defined covering floors 4–9, ensuring players encounter them in roughly the second half of their first tier.
implementation: data/monsters.ts: NamedVariant interface {id, name, baseId, forcedAffixes, minFloor, hpBonus}. NAMED_VARIANTS constant (8 entries). getNamedVariantsForFloor(floor) returns variants where floor >= minFloor. engine/monsters.ts/spawnMonster: eligibleVariants = tier!=='normal' ? getNamedVariantsForFloor(floor) : []. If eligibleVariants.length>0 && rng()<0.20: pick variant, set def=MONSTERS.find(m=>m.id===variant.baseId), affixes=variant.forcedAffixes, displayName=variant.name, extraHpMult=variant.hpBonus. Otherwise random as before. After instance creation, applyAffixModifiers runs on forcedAffixes the same as random ones — stat bonuses from extraStrong/extraFast still apply on top of hpBonus. Current 8 variants: Plagueborn Zombie (zombie, coldEnchanted+cursed, floor 4, ×1.20), Soulfire Imp (imp, fireEnchanted+teleporting, floor 4, ×1.10), Voidborn Fallen (fallen, lightningEnchanted+extraFast, floor 5, ×1.15), Ironbone Warrior (bone_warrior, extraStrong+aura, floor 5, ×1.25), Bloodrage Hawk (blood_hawk, extraFast+extraStrong, floor 6, ×1.20), Blighted Shaman (dark_shaman, cursed+coldEnchanted, floor 7, ×1.30), Stone Sentinel (golem, aura+extraStrong, floor 8, ×1.30), Void Wraith (wraith, teleporting+cursed, floor 9, ×1.25).

decisions.decision: Named variants replace random affix generation rather than adding to it
decisions.rationale: Allowing named variants to spawn with random affixes on top of forcedAffixes would create unpredictable power spikes (e.g. Ironbone Warrior + extraStrong × 2). The variant's identity comes from its fixed combo — a cold-immune zombie that curses your defense is a specific challenge, not a base for further randomization.
alternativesConsidered: Variant + 1 random extra affix — adds variance but breaks the named identity promise
Separate spawns alongside random elites — doubles elite encounter rate
decisions.recommendation: If variant selected: affixes = forcedAffixes only. No random roll. applyAffixModifiers still runs for stat effects.

decisions.decision: 20% spawn chance for elite+ tiers, not 100%
decisions.rationale: If named variants were guaranteed, players would know the exact affix combo of every elite — removing the tension of the random elite encounters that still appear 80% of the time. 20% makes named variants a notable-but-not-constant occurrence: a few per run, memorable when they appear.
alternativesConsidered: 50% — too frequent, named variants dominate elite encounter variety
5% — too rare, most players won't encounter them within a typical run
decisions.recommendation: 20% via rng() < 0.20. Stack-agnostic: seeded RNG call, deterministic per run.

decisions.decision: hpBonus and applyAffixModifiers both apply to variant HP
decisions.rationale: A named variant with extraStrong (which grants +20% HP via applyAffixModifiers) stacking with hpBonus could be seen as double-dipping. However, the HP bonus from the affix is part of what makes the affix meaningful. Removing it for named variants would make an extraStrong variant feel weaker than an extraStrong random elite, breaking player intuition.
alternativesConsidered: Skip applyAffixModifiers HP bonus for variants — inconsistent with random elite behavior
decisions.recommendation: Let both apply. Named variants are intended to be more threatening than same-level random elites — the compound scaling is by design.

successCriteria: Named variants appear in elite/rare/ancient encounters at ~20% rate from floor 4+. Each variant always has exactly its forcedAffixes and no others. displayName matches variant.name (not the generic affix-prefix format). hpBonus multiplier visible in increased maxHp vs. base monster. All 8 variants accessible in sim scripts. TypeScript 0 errors.
knownLimitations: getNamedVariantsForFloor eligibility is based only on minFloor — variants can appear indefinitely on higher floors (e.g. Plagueborn Zombie can spawn on floor 9). No maxFloor implemented.
No variant tracking in careerStats — players cannot see which named variants they have encountered
Only 8 variants for 10 floors in tier 1 — some floors (floor 1-3, floor 10) have no eligible variants, making all their elites random
userGuide:
## Named Variant Enemies

Starting on floor 4, elite and rare encounters have a 20% chance to spawn a **Named Variant** — a specific enemy with a fixed identity and affix combination. Unlike random elites, named variants always have the same mechanics, so you can prepare once you know them.

| Name | Base | Affixes | Floor |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plagueborn Zombie | Zombie | Cold Enchanted + Cursed | 4+ |
| Soulfire Imp | Imp | Fire Enchanted + Teleporting | 4+ |
| Voidborn Fallen | Fallen | Lightning Enchanted + Extra Fast | 5+ |
| Ironbone Warrior | Bone Warrior | Extra Strong + Aura | 5+ |
| Bloodrage Hawk | Blood Hawk | Extra Fast + Extra Strong | 6+ |
| Blighted Shaman | Dark Shaman | Cursed + Cold Enchanted | 7+ |
| Stone Sentinel | Golem | Aura + Extra Strong | 8+ |
| Void Wraith | Wraith | Teleporting + Cursed | 9+ |

### Key threats to know
- **Plagueborn Zombie**: Cold Enchanted means it deals cold damage on hits AND resists cold spells. Cursed halves your defense. High-priority target — kill fast.
- **Ironbone Warrior + Stone Sentinel**: Both have Aura + Extra Strong, making them exceptionally tanky and hard-hitting. Warrior's Battle Cry or Rogue's Shadow Step negates the attack turn.
- **Void Wraith**: Teleporting means chill/freeze hit penalties and Smoke Bomb are both ignored. Cursed halves your defense. Pure damage race — no kiting.
componentFile: src/data/monsters.ts (NamedVariant, NAMED_VARIANTS, getNamedVariantsForFloor), src/engine/monsters.ts (spawnMonster variant selection branch)

publicInterface.name: NamedVariant
publicInterface.description: { id: string, name: string, baseId: string, forcedAffixes: MonsterAffix[], minFloor: number, hpBonus: number }

publicInterface.name: NAMED_VARIANTS
publicInterface.description: All 8 named variant definitions, exported from data/monsters.ts

publicInterface.name: getNamedVariantsForFloor
publicInterface.description: Returns all variants eligible on the given floor (floor >= minFloor)
